release.action:
Features:
  • Added dedicated embedding provider abstraction and manager lookup for improved embedding model support.
  • Added support for multi-line input with line breaker and user signature in readLine method.
  • Added instructions and model parameter handling in ActMojo for Maven plugin execution.
Fixes:
  • Fixed classification JSON detection in Picker and updated embedding model provider prefix in assembly act.
  • Fixed rclone command syntax in site.toml to use backticks for Windows compatibility.
  • Fixed null checks to prevent overwriting instructions and model properties in ActProcessor.
  • Fixed fallback to System.out.print for null console and added model logging in Ghostwriter.
  • Fixed missing return statement in embedding method of CodeMieProvider.
Refactoring:
  • Refactored embedding support across OpenAI and CodeMie providers with dedicated EmbeddingProvider type validation.
  • Refactored CodeMieProvider to use named inner classes for provider extensions.
  • Refactored provider initialization to pass model name as explicit parameter instead of through configuration.
  • Refactored bindex act to use multi-step inputs with dedicated javadoc generation.
  • Refactored maven-javadoc-plugin configuration from reporting to pluginManagement section.
  • Removed unused imports, dependencies, and methods across multiple modules.

Features:
  • Added Claude provider implementation with tool-call handling, structured message processing, beta API integration, and web search support.
  • Improved provider resolution and model routing for CodeMie and Claude-backed configurations.
Fixes:
  • Fixed cache input token calculation in ClaudeProvider.
  • Removed the deprecated NoneProvider from provider routing.
Refactoring:
  • Refactored shared provider infrastructure into a common abstract base for OpenAI and Claude integrations, including MCP server and web search configuration handling.
  • Refactored function call handling and per-model usage statistics management across provider implementations.
  • Updated function tools and provider manager internals for improved provider resolution and tool execution behavior.

Features:
  • Added WebSearchTool support for web search integration.
  • Added MCP server support, including helper methods for web search and MCP server registration.
  • Implemented support for multiple MCP server definitions.
  • Added support for gemini- family models in the CodeMie provider.
Fixes:
  • Set reasoning defaults to prevent reasoning-related issues, including ReasoningEffort.NONE handling for supported models.
  • Fixed multi-MCP registration handling for repeated addMcpServer() definitions.
  • Renamed ProcessTerminationException to TaskTerminationException for clearer task termination semantics.
  • Updated recursive file discovery to use ProjectLayout.findFiles(directory) and include excluded directory handling.
Documentation:
  • Updated Javadoc and release notes.
  • Extended system and final instructions to include excluded directory configuration such as .machai.

Tests and Maintenance

  • Added tests and addressed multiple sonar issues.
  • Performed CodeMie provider cleanup and related maintenance updates.
  • Released version 1.1.8.

Features:
  • Added a unified GenAI provider API for prompt instructions, execution, embeddings, tool registration, working-directory context, cleanup, and usage reporting.
  • Added provider resolution through GenaiProviderManager, including Provider:model selectors, fully qualified provider classes, provider initialization, and aggregated token usage.
  • Added an OpenAI-compatible provider with Responses API execution, function tool calling, input logging, timeout and output-token configuration, embeddings, and usage mapping.
  • Added EPAM CodeMie integration with OAuth token acquisition and delegation to an OpenAI-compatible backend.
  • Added a no-op provider for disabled, offline, test, and prompt-capture scenarios.
  • Added extensible functional tools support with FunctionTools, FunctionToolsLoader, ToolFunction, Java ServiceLoader discovery, configurable placeholder resolution, and working-directory-aware tool execution.
